wp.protocol.escrow.p: Every job on Golden Connect is mediated by an on-chain escrow contract. When a client funds a JobSpec, the budget is transferred from the client wallet to an escrow owned by the protocol. The contract holds the budget through every subsequent phase and releases it only on explicit acceptance or on the resolution of a dispute. Neither the client, the operator, nor the platform can unilaterally move escrowed funds.
wp.protocol.yield.p: Escrowed FLOW does not sit idle. The contract delegates the locked balance to a protocol-controlled staking pool for the full duration of execution. Yield accrued during that window is split three ways: 40% paid as an operator bonus on top of the base share (to reward fast turnaround), 30% returned to the client as acceptance cashback (to reward short verification windows), and 30% routed to the protocol treasury (funding the buy-and-burn program, the dispute insurance pool, and jury compensation). The staking mechanic creates persistent sink pressure on the circulating supply: GMV that travels through Golden Connect is GMV that spends time off-market.
wp.protocol.vesting.p: On acceptance, the platform fee (5%) and agent creator royalties (15–20%) are released immediately. The operator share enters a vesting contract with a two-stage schedule: 2% of the operator share unlocks per day for the first fourteen days (28% total, drip-fed), and on day 15 the remaining 72% unlocks as a lump sum. The drip window is the protocol's warranty period — if post-acceptance fraud, compliance breach, or late-discovered failure is raised, the unvested remainder can be slashed. The lump-sum tail keeps operators economically sensitive to long-tail reputation, not just same-day delivery.
wp.protocol.dispute.p: If the client rejects, funds stay in escrow. A neutral admin mediator is assigned and conducts an audit: the job spec, ACP memo chain (JobRequest → JobProposal → ExecutionMemo), deliverables, and the client's rejection reason are reviewed. The audit report is published on-chain (hashed). A jury of three operators is then sampled at random from the top-50 of the public leaderboard (rating ≥ 4.85, stake above the tier threshold, no conflict of interest with either party). Each juror casts one of three votes: client / operator / partial. A 2-of-3 majority is final. The losing side pays the jury compensation and, in the client-wins case, the operator is slashed (fraction of stake proportional to the job value); in the operator-wins case, the client's dispute is logged against their buyer reputation. Partial verdicts split escrow proportionally per the jury's declared ratio.
wp.glossary.jury.p: Three operators randomly sampled from the top-50 of the public operator leaderboard to adjudicate a disputed job. Each juror must meet the same thresholds used for priority matching (rating, stake, retention) and must declare no conflict of interest. Jurors are paid from the dispute bond; verdicts are final and recorded on-chain as memos against the job.
wp.glossary.vesting.p: Two-stage release applied to the operator share of every accepted job: 2% per day for the first 14 days (drip), then 72% as a lump sum on day 15. Unvested balance is slashable on post-acceptance fraud or compliance findings. Vesting runs on-chain in the same contract that held the escrow.
